finding_results.notes: Two targeted edits to the early-morning kitchen exchange (lines 117 and 121). Cliff's 'That is a hell of a phrase' corrected to 'That's a hell of a thing to say' — contraction restored, 'phrase' replaced with the more colloquial 'thing,' and 'before anybody's even called' adds natural contracted auxiliary. Joel's composed punchline ('Unfortunately, I do' + balanced 'locker room / team meeting' metaphor) replaced with a self-interrupting redirect: 'Yeah, we do. And — it's not the time, Tommy. You're coaching.' The dash marks genuine mid-thought abandonment; 'it's not the time' centers grief over diagnosis; 'You're coaching' is a flat two-word observation using shorthand the group would have for Tommy's behavior, not a balanced rhetorical construction. Nate's grief-static line at 123 left untouched — already alive. The later rebuke cluster (snapshot lines 149-163: 'coaching witnesses' / 'football speeches and legal seminars' / 'pregame') was already replaced by a prior revision with Joel's practical bathroom-timing warning and Tommy's internal realization, eliminating the three-consecutive-polished-restatement problem at that location.

summary: Single finding addressed with two line-level dialogue edits. Cliff's stiff uncontracted 'That is' returned to his moderate-contraction transactional register. Joel's mic-ready two-part punchline broken into a self-interrupting redirect that names the behavior flatly rather than diagnosing it with balanced metaphor. The post-deputy rebuke cluster (snapshot 149-163) had already been replaced by a prior pass, so no further changes were needed there. The revised sequence at lines 117-123 now delivers three distinct reactions — Cliff's colloquial observation, Joel's friction-laden redirect, Nate's grief-exhaustion — without queuing up polished variations on the same joke.
